questions.prompt: What is Spring Boot auto-configuration trying to do?

answers.text: Auto-configuration is conditional on the application environment.

feedback.legit: Correct. Auto-configuration is conditional on the application environment.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Auto-configuration is conditional on the application environment.

answers.text: User-defined beans can cause many defaults to back off.

feedback.legit: Correct. User-defined beans can cause many defaults to back off.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. User-defined beans can cause many defaults to back off.

answers.text: Configure common beans based on the classpath, properties, and existing beans.

feedback.legit: Correct. Configure common beans based on the classpath, properties, and existing beans.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Configure common beans based on the classpath, properties, and existing beans.

questions.prompt: What is a Spring Boot starter?

answers.text: Starters reduce the need to select every transitive library manually.

feedback.legit: Correct. Starters reduce the need to select every transitive library manually.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Starters reduce the need to select every transitive library manually.

answers.text: A curated dependency descriptor for a common capability.

feedback.legit: Correct. A curated dependency descriptor for a common capability.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. A curated dependency descriptor for a common capability.

answers.text: A running application server managed outside the project.

feedback.legit: Not quite. A starter is a dependency bundle, not a separate server process.
feedback.sus: Correct. A starter is a dependency bundle, not a separate server process.

questions.prompt: Can a Spring Boot web application run with an embedded server?

answers.text: Yes, it can package and launch a server with the application.

feedback.legit: Correct. Yes, it can package and launch a server with the application.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Yes, it can package and launch a server with the application.

answers.text: No, every deployment requires a separately installed application server.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Executable Spring Boot applications commonly use an embedded server.
feedback.sus: Correct. Executable Spring Boot applications commonly use an embedded server.

answers.text: An embedded server is suitable only for local development.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Embedded servers are commonly used in production when deployed and operated appropriately.
feedback.sus: Correct. Embedded servers are commonly used in production when deployed and operated appropriately.

questions.prompt: Why is constructor injection often preferred?

answers.text: Constructor parameters make dependencies straightforward to supply in tests.

feedback.legit: Correct. Constructor parameters make dependencies straightforward to supply in tests.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Constructor parameters make dependencies straightforward to supply in tests.

answers.text: A single constructor can be used without an explicit Autowired annotation.

feedback.legit: Correct. A single constructor can be used without an explicit Autowired annotation.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. A single constructor can be used without an explicit Autowired annotation.

answers.text: Required dependencies are explicit and can be final.

feedback.legit: Correct. Required dependencies are explicit and can be final.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Required dependencies are explicit and can be final.

answers.text: Active profiles can be selected through external configuration.

feedback.legit: Correct. Active profiles can be selected through external configuration.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Active profiles can be selected through external configuration.

answers.text: Conditional activation of beans and configuration for named environments.

feedback.legit: Correct. Conditional activation of beans and configuration for named environments.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Conditional activation of beans and configuration for named environments.

answers.text: Compile-time Java language features.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Profiles are runtime configuration and bean-selection features.
feedback.sus: Correct. Profiles are runtime configuration and bean-selection features.

questions.prompt: What does Spring Boot Actuator add?

answers.text: Operational endpoints and production-oriented application information.

feedback.legit: Correct. Operational endpoints and production-oriented application information.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Operational endpoints and production-oriented application information.

answers.text: Automatic public exposure of every endpoint is always safe.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Endpoint exposure and security must be configured deliberately.
feedback.sus: Correct. Endpoint exposure and security must be configured deliberately.

answers.text: A healthy Actuator response proves every external dependency is healthy.

feedback.legit: Not quite. A health result covers only the indicators that are configured and included.
feedback.sus: Correct. A health result covers only the indicators that are configured and included.

questions.prompt: Why can self-invocation bypass `@Transactional` in Spring's default proxy mode?

answers.text: Transactions only work on static methods.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Proxy interception, not static dispatch, is the key issue.
feedback.sus: Correct. Proxy interception, not static dispatch, is the key issue.

answers.text: Moving the call across beans or using another transaction mechanism can restore interception.

feedback.legit: Correct. Moving the call across beans or using another transaction mechanism can restore interception.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Moving the call across beans or using another transaction mechanism can restore interception.

answers.text: A call through `this` does not pass through the Spring proxy.

feedback.legit: Correct. A call through `this` does not pass through the Spring proxy.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. A call through `this` does not pass through the Spring proxy.

questions.prompt: What is the default scope of a Spring bean?

answers.text: Web-aware contexts also provide request and session scopes.

feedback.legit: Correct. Web-aware contexts also provide request and session scopes.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Web-aware contexts also provide request and session scopes.

answers.text: Singleton within the application context.

feedback.legit: Correct. Singleton within the application context.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Singleton within the application context.

answers.text: A new instance for every method call.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Prototype creates new instances on lookup; singleton is the default.
feedback.sus: Correct. Prototype creates new instances on lookup; singleton is the default.

questions.prompt: Can the same Spring Boot property come from multiple sources?

answers.text: Yes, defined precedence rules decide which value wins.

feedback.legit: Correct. Yes, defined precedence rules decide which value wins.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Yes, defined precedence rules decide which value wins.

answers.text: Only `application.properties` can supply configuration.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Environment variables, command-line arguments, and other sources are supported.
feedback.sus: Correct. Environment variables, command-line arguments, and other sources are supported.

answers.text: When two property sources define the same key, the lower-priority source always wins.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Spring Boot uses defined property-source precedence, and the higher-priority value wins.
feedback.sus: Correct. Spring Boot uses defined property-source precedence, and the higher-priority value wins.

questions.prompt: What is needed for Bean Validation annotations to reject invalid request data?

answers.text: Annotations validate every object automatically when it is constructed.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Constraints are checked when a validator is invoked through the relevant integration.
feedback.sus: Correct. Constraints are checked when a validator is invoked through the relevant integration.

answers.text: Binding errors should be translated into a clear client response.

feedback.legit: Correct. Binding errors should be translated into a clear client response.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Binding errors should be translated into a clear client response.

answers.text: Validation must be triggered, commonly with `@Valid` or `@Validated`.

feedback.legit: Correct. Validation must be triggered, commonly with `@Valid` or `@Validated`.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Validation must be triggered, commonly with `@Valid` or `@Validated`.

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of a Spring Boot test slice?

answers.text: `@WebMvcTest` focuses on MVC components rather than the full application.

feedback.legit: Correct. `@WebMvcTest` focuses on MVC components rather than the full application.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. `@WebMvcTest` focuses on MVC components rather than the full application.

answers.text: Load a focused subset of application configuration for one layer.

feedback.legit: Correct. Load a focused subset of application configuration for one layer.
feedback.sus: This answer is legitimate. Load a focused subset of application configuration for one layer.

answers.text: Start every external production dependency.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Slices intentionally narrow the context and often use test replacements.
feedback.sus: Correct. Slices intentionally narrow the context and often use test replacements.

questions.prompt: What does graceful shutdown aim to do?

answers.text: Graceful shutdown makes every interrupted request safe to retry.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Retry safety depends on the operation's idempotency and application design, not shutdown behavior alone.
feedback.sus: Correct. Retry safety depends on the operation's idempotency and application design, not shutdown behavior alone.

answers.text: Terminate the process immediately without notifying the server.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Immediate termination can drop active work.
feedback.sus: Correct. Immediate termination can drop active work.

answers.text: Graceful shutdown waits forever for every request by default.

feedback.legit: Not quite. Graceful shutdown is bounded by configured application and infrastructure timeouts.
feedback.sus: Correct. Graceful shutdown is bounded by configured application and infrastructure timeouts.
